title: insunis
text: insunis When you install Uniface on Unix, the installation program generates a shell script called insunis that defines Uniface’s environment variables. Default location: UnifaceAPSInstallDir /common/adm Description Table 1. Environment Variables Defined in insunis Environment Variable Description urouter urouter.sh (Unix and Linux) —Uniface Router executable uniface Uniface executable urm Resource manager executable USYS Directory containing Uniface system components USYSADM Directory containing Uniface assignment files; can be overridden with the /adm flag USYSBIN Directory containing Uniface executables USYSLIB Directory containing dynamically loadable libraries, or shared objects USYSINS Uniface installation directory USYSCOM common directory USYSUNIFACE uniface directory Unix To set the environment variables, insunis must be executed from either the /etc/profile file or the individual user’s .profile file. To set these variables globally, ensure that the /etc/profile file contains the following: . { UnifaceInstallationDirectory }/common/insunis[ Id ] Copy Related Links /adm Specify the directory where the application's configuration files are located.
